reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on October 19, 2024
reviewText: I bought this for my Great Dane's nails because clippers always upset her and, at almost 12 now, getting her to the vet for nail trim appointments was both stressful for her and expensive for me. This Dremel is about the same cost as one nail trim appointment so I figured why not give it a shot. What a difference this has made!! With clippers, it would take two people to get her nails done with one to hold her still and the other to trim the nails. With this Dremel, she let me trim her nails all on my own without help. And I have never been able to do that before. It was like a spa day for her just laying down while I learned how to carefully trim each nail. If you have a big dog like me, you'll need to completely remove the protective cover to have a large enough surface area for the nails. And, as the directions and others have commented, be careful with any long hairs around your pet or yourself to avoid injury. For someone who has always been nervous about trimming dog nails, I was able to do this easily in one shot. The Dremel is super quiet and does require patience to trim the nails so give it time and lots of treats. Unfortunately, my bulldog is terrified of it and won't let it anywhere near his feet. reviewText: I’m not normally a “write a review after one use” person. I like to test it out, see how it lasts. But with this tool I immediately thought it was worth it. They are AWFUL for their nail trims and I usually push it off way too long. My husband used my old grinder and forgot where he put it, I pushed off their trims too long so I needed a new one cheap and fast in case I found my expensive one. And here we are. After using this grinder I’m amazed. It’s the best one I’ve ever used. This is the best both dogs have ever sat. I think that is largely due to how quiet this is. Both dogs are noise reactive and this is the quietest grinder I’ve ever used, just a gentle buzzing you can definitely still have a conversation while it’s on. I like that the head is slightly concave. It seems to keep in the nail in place so you don’t skip and hit their paw pad as much. It came with a safety head. I have big dogs, so I removed that, it wouldn’t have worked with their nails. It is also really small and light and easy to grip and maneuver. I can’t say anything about the longevity of the product. I will update if it conks out in the near future. So no update means it’s still going strong. It’s currently June 2024. If need a cheap nail grinder this one is going to go above and beyond!

reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on July 18, 2023
reviewText: Our dogs really really hate getting their nails trimmed. They run, claw up our arms trying to escape, and twist their bodies so much I often worry I might break a paw if not careful. The biggest issue is that Yorkies have black nails so you cannot see the quick. Clipping the nails of a dog that is freaking out is not easy and often ends up with them bleeding and everyone upset as a result. Clipping only a tony bit to avoid risking the quick means having to clip more often and more struggling. The grinder is pretty quick without risking the quick. I combined this with a grooming harness so they can't run and just hang there while their nails are grinded down. This grinder was essential for one dew claw that was so guarded it was starting to touch his skin so using normal cutters were impossible as it was guaranteed to cut his skin. With the grinder, harness, and a cup of water to wet his fur to keep it out of the way we finally got the dew claw to a normal length. We tried getting them used to the noise it made, but the noise changes when it touches the nail and is not silent so frankly that was a lost cause. They didn't care about the grinder until it got close to them. The noise isn't that bad really and I doubt they could make something like this much quieter. I also liked that there was a cap to prevent damage to the grinder and the nail guard came off so it was easy to clean. I was able to turn the device on and off with one hand so perfect for finicky paws. Overall the most important thing was that this was so much safer than normal clippers. We haven't drawn blood even once so far with this.

reviewText: I got this for our pit mix who kiiiilllllsssss us with his claws and it’s UH-MAY-ZING!!! reviewTitle: Get off the fence and buy it.
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2021
reviewText: This thing is AWESOME! I don't leave reviews as standard practice, however, I am compelled to compliment this device. It is very quiet on low speed, slightly louder on high. Emits more of a hum than a whine. The motor is extremely powerful, even on low speed. It doesn't slow when grinding, so it's easy to remove as much or as little material as you want to. It spins the diamond grit head so fast on low speed that vibration transmitted to the dog's nail is minimum and the dogs don't twitch even half as much. High speed is for experienced users. The 3.6 volt battery charges fast. The voltage input spec is 5V, 1000mV. It does not come with a wall charger, but it does come with charging cord. The removable guard on this unit works well, and will help new users learn to control cutting to deep too quickly. As I have experience with this type of tool, I use it without the guard. The compact size, high speed, and contours of the grinding head, made it a simple task to shape and buff the claws smooth on the ends. It can also cut out the sides of the claw which hold the mud. In my analysis, I give this 5 stars. If you're on the fence about purchasing this unit, don't hesitate to buy it.

I love how fast and easy it is to file off any rough edges - I usually trim with clippers first and then use the nail grinder to buff away edges and get closer to the quick. reviewTitle: WARNING** Long-haired owners tie back hair/loose articles before use. DONT  USE ON LONG HAIRED PETS.
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on September 23, 2023
reviewText: I gave this product three stars. The reason being is because the product does file pets nails relatively less stressfully, as when a dog nail clippers are in use- if done safely and properly. The product does seem to be of relatively good quality/materials. That... I gave this product three stars. The reason being is because the product does file pets nails relatively less stressfully, as when a dog nail clippers are in use- if done safely and properly. The product does seem to be of relatively good quality/materials. That being said, my concern lies in the fact that there are virtually zero safety precautions for material or hair entanglement regarding the presence of any automatic shut off when increased resistance is met. Most other products with rotating components (I.e. handheld fans, vacuum cleaners) can detect if a snag is detected at which point the motor stops spinning and shuts off until the device is cleared of snags and restarted. In this situation, a single strand of my hair came in contact with rotating end of the dog nail grinder, and it started twisting until an entire chunk of my hair became fully entangled, which happened over the course of approximately 1-2 seconds. The device continued spinning and spinning and I could not get the sliding on/off switch to slide off because the device continue to spin in my hand. The device was very hot, almost smoking, because of the amount of hair entangling, but it did not turn off. This had me greatly concerned, not just for myself, but for my dog. Because my dog is a long-haired mixed breed (Bernedoodle, or Poodle and Bernese Mountain Dog mix), her hair could easily become stuck in the twisting mechanism. Especially because she is also a large breed, meaning we cannot use the included plastic nail guard due to the size and shape of her nails. At which point, this would be much a more painful and traumatizing experience than just using a regular nail clipper, and I do not want to traumatize her… or myself. I feel like if the rotary mechanism senses a snag or a tangle in the rotating bit, it should have a safety feature that automatically shuts it off so that people and/or their pets do not have hair (or other loose articles) incidentally damaged, burned, ripped out, or removed. The reason I gave 3 stars instead of 1 is because I understand that this issue is partially my fault and in the future, I would tie my hair back fully if I were to use it again. Also it’s not an entirely useless product, but can only be safely used (in my opinion) on smaller, shorter haired breeds. With that being said, I am glad this happened to me rather than my pet because I was able to easily cut the affected strands of my hair that were burned and frayed off and am physically unharmed, although my hair does look very strange now since this large strand was in the very front. Regardless, I take some responsibility for the incident, however, I don’t want my situation to happen to any long haired pets in the future and hope the company makes some safety adjustments to the product. One thing that could significantly improve safety would be to include an overall larger grinding bit that also includes a larger plastic safety guard with a bigger opening for large breed dogs, so that their nails can be filed safely with decreased risk for tangling, ripping, tugging, etc. As mentioned, I was not able to use the provided guard that comes with the product because my dogs nails are simply too wide/thick to keep the guard on. If you have a small pet with thinner nails, the guard would likely better protect you/your pet or reduce the risk of entanglement by probably 90%. In conclusion, I will likely not use this on my long haired dogs, but I would be willing to try the product again on my short haired, smaller dog (Boston terrier) because the risk for entanglement is minimal to none (for the pet), and I can use the file guard that is included with the product. Of course, my hair will be tied back next time… I hope this helps someone make a more informed decision on purchasing this product.

reviewTitle: Affordable? Yes. Well-Designed? Not So Much.
reviewedIn: Reviewed in the United States on January 13, 2025
reviewText: We had this nail grinder for a few months, and it worked well for us to be able to round off the long or sharp edges of our Boston terrier Gypsy’s nails. One charge generally would get us through 2-2.5 trims, depending on whether we were doing only Gypsy or our aunt’s Labrador, Parker, also. It worked well, and besides one incident where Gypsy’s dew claw became lodged between the file head and its guard (by God’s grace, we were able to get it free with no blood or excessive pain for her), it never failed us. Until, that is, I went to charge it one day, noticing the motor spinning was not as fast as it should be, and then I left it on the charger for 2.75 days. A busy life and ADD will do that to you. When I went to get it 2.75 days later to trim Gypsy’s nails, I noticed a problem. After a supposed full charge, it quickly seemed to run low on battery, as I was only able to just barely do her front two paws before the motor rotation became noticeably slower. My wonderful Dad, an electrical engineer, had explained this phenomenon to me before with a cordless automatic hair curler. When a cord-free rechargeable device does not have an automatic charging shut-off mechanism, which all quality ones should have, it overcharges the battery, wears it out, and causes it to hold less charge than before, and if it continues to happen, it will cause it to not hold any charge at all. The same problem that took out my favorite hair curler had now ruined a perfectly good nail grinder. My word of advice to the manufacturer is to put a little more money into the device to add an auto-shut off feature when it reaches full charge. I’d be happier to pay a little more for a quality product that will last because I’d not have to set a timer to go unplug my charging dog nail grinder; you’d be happier because you’d keep me as a happy customer instead of me now spending money with a different manufacturer because your design flaw caused me to have to replace my dog nail grinder.
